scenarios.label: Oncology Access / Operational Constraint
scenarios.hcpUtterance: We are already short on chair time and staffing, so feasibility matters.
repResponses: I hear chair time is tight and staffing is stretched. We can start with one 10-minute infusion-slot checklist this week.
To keep workflow light, we can pilot with two patients and track turnaround impact only.
If this still adds burden today, we can pause and revisit when your team has capacity.

cueSnippets: already moving toward a patient room
already moving toward a patient room

scenarios.label: Clinical Evidence Threshold
scenarios.hcpUtterance: I need stronger evidence quality before changing practice.
repResponses: Understood. A randomized phase 3 subgroup showed a 14% absolute response lift with similar grade 3 adverse events.
If useful, I can share the endpoint definition and confidence interval so your team can validate applicability.
Given your threshold, one practical next step is reviewing that dataset in tumor board this week.

scenarios.label: Time-Pressured / High-Volume Clinic
scenarios.hcpUtterance: I have two minutes. Keep this concise.
repResponses: I will keep this to one point: same-day ordering reduced treatment-start delays by four days in a similar clinic.
No extra deck—just one workflow change and one metric to monitor this week.
If that is not useful right now, we can stop here and I will follow your preferred timing.
